mainEntity.name: How much does a Discovery Sport engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Discovery Sport engine typically costs £2,800–£5,800 supply only [EM-QUOTE], with the volume 204DTD diesel at £3,500–£5,800. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Discovery Sport engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 204PT Ingenium petrol and post-2019 204DTD diesels. The 2019.5 facelift's revised chain hardware materially changed the diesel's reliability profile.

mainEntity.name: Which Discovery Sport engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but a pre-2019 204DTD diesel without a documented timing-chain inspection is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The engine is fine; the unknown is not.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my Discovery Sport timing chain is worn?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle for the first few seconds is the classic early warning, sometimes with a chain-noise change under load. Caught early it's a repair; ignored, it's a £3,500–£5,800 engine [EM-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Sport engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Usually yes — £3,500–£5,800 against typical £10,000+ values clears our 60% scrap-threshold rule. The honest exception is the cheapest 2015–2016 cars, where a worst-case quote brushes the threshold. Use the Ownership Decision Tool for your car.

mainEntity.name: Which year Discovery Sport should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: 2020 or later. You get the facelift architecture, mild-hybrid diesels with revised chain hardware, and the P300e option for short-run drivers. Among pre-facelift cars, insist on chain-inspection paperwork.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery Sport engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; the block stamping is accessible from below on the Ingenium units. Or tell us your variant (TD4, D180, P250 etc.) and we'll identify it.

mainEntity.name: Discovery Sport vs Evoque — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanically they're siblings — same platform family, same Ingenium engines, same pre/post-2019 story. The Discovery Sport adds the 5+2 seats; reliability differences are trim-deep. Buy on year and history, not badge.

hero.h1: Discovery Sport Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine. Every known failure. Every replacement cost. The most-enquired Land Rover in our entire dataset — backed by 362+ real UK Discovery Sport enquiries [EM-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ✅ Usually Worth Replacing — £3,500–£5,800 recon [EM-QUOTE] against typical £8,000–£45,000 host values clears our 60% rule on all but the very cheapest early diesels ⚠️
★★★☆☆ — one model, two lives: pre-2019 builds carry the Ingenium chain caveat, 2019.5MY+ builds are markedly stronger
2020+ D165/D180 MHEV — post-facelift architecture, revised chain hardware
P300e PHEV (2020+) for short-run drivers who'd otherwise kill a diesel's DPF
📈 Most-Enquired Model in the UK — 362 enquiries (2025) [EM-VERIFIED]

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The Discovery Sport is Britain's default family Land Rover and the single most-enquired model in our data — not because it's the worst engine, but because there are so many of them and the pre-2019 timing chain question hangs over every used listing.

generations.h2: Discovery Sport — One Generation, Two Lives
generations.subHeadline: Every Discovery Sport is an L550 — but a 2016 and a 2021 car are very different ownership propositions.

cards.title: Discovery Sport (L550)
cards.verdict: Buy on the facelift line, not the badge. 2019.5MY brought the revised architecture, mild-hybrid drivetrains and the improved chain hardware that changed this model's reliability story.

engineDatabase.h2: Discovery Sport Engine Codes — The Complete Database

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: 204DTD rating: 95% confidence — the single most-enquired engine in our dataset (1,150 JLR-wide) plus 362 model-level L550 enquiries [EM-VERIFIED]. The split rating (pre/post-2019) reflects a genuine hardware change, not opinion. 224DT, Si4 and P300e: Expert-knowledge tier — documented patterns and specialist reports; the P300e parc is simply too young for volume data. Data note (internal): Correction applied: the source data sheet omitted the Ford-derived Si4 GTDi petrol (2015–2017, pre-Ingenium) — added as pattern data so early petrol owners aren't orphaned. 224DT years corrected to 2015–2016 (launch engine, replaced by Ingenium within the first year); the sheet's "2.2 Duratorq" listing implied longer availability.

problems.description: The defining Discovery Sport issue and our #1 enquiry driver on this model [EM-VERIFIED]. Cold-start rattle is the warning; left alone, chain slip destroys the engine. Pre-2019 builds. £3,500–£5,800 recon [EM-QUOTE].
The school-run problem: short journeys interrupt DPF regeneration, fuel dilutes the oil, wear accelerates. The single biggest reason we point short-run drivers at the P300e.
Coolant loss and rough running on higher-mileage Ingenium diesels; repairable if caught before overheating causes head damage.
The launch-era Duratorq's known weak points, carried over from Freelander 2 service life. Well understood, moderate cost.
Not an engine failure, but the most common non-engine enquiry on the L550 — power transfer unit wear on pre-facelift cars, often misdiagnosed as driveline vibration.
The Ford-derived petrol's age-related weak points — worth a specialist check on any surviving 2015–2017 petrol car.

marketIntelligence.h2: Discovery Sport Market Intelligence — What 362 UK Owners Told Us in 2025
insights: Interesting insight: The Discovery Sport generates more engine enquiries than any other single Land Rover — more than models costing three times as much. Volume of cars on UK roads plus the pre-2019 chain question is a demand engine nobody else can measure.

L550 enquiries skew heavily toward the 2015–2018 diesel parc now exiting affordable-warranty age — the exact cars where a £4,000+ engine decision meets a £10,000–£14,000 vehicle. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)
Petrol and PHEV enquiry volume remains minimal — consistent with the smaller petrol parc, not evidence of superior reliability alone. (Editorial synthesis.)
marketIntelligence.liveEnquiryFeedNote: Live Enquiry Feed module embeds here.

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every Discovery Sport Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one Discovery Sport based on this page, buy a 2020-or-later car. The 2019.5 facelift is the dividing line between this model's two reliability lives — and on a pre-facelift diesel, a documented timing-chain inspection is worth more than any service stamp.

replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: 204DTD and 204PT figures [EM-QUOTE]; 224DT, Si4 and P300e [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs Table A].
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery Sport engine typically takes 10–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].

economicsBox.title: The Discovery Sport rule
economicsBox.text: A £3,500–£5,800 recon against a £10,000+ car clears our 60% scrap-threshold rule comfortably — replacement is ✅ usually safe. The exception is the cheapest 2015–2016 diesels now trading at £7,000–£9,000: there, a top-end recon quote brushes the threshold, and a chain-damaged engine on a tired car is where we'll honestly tell you to walk away.

engineEvolution.h2: How the Discovery Sport Engine Range Evolved — 2015 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: The Freelander's replacement launched on borrowed engines — Ford-derived diesel and petrol as a bridge
eras.worthKnowing: The 2.2 lasted barely a year in the range; survivors are now the cheapest Discovery Sports on sale

eras.whyBmwChanged: JLR's first in-house modular engine family arrived here first — the Discovery Sport was Ingenium's proving ground
eras.worthKnowing: Being first meant inheriting the early chain-tensioner hardware; this era defines the model's used-market reputation

eras.whyBmwChanged: Emissions targets drove electrification — mild-hybrid diesels, then the three-cylinder PHEV
eras.worthKnowing: The same facelift quietly brought the revised chain hardware — the reliability story changed more than the styling did

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a Discovery Sport?

profiles.verdict: The affordable 5+2-seat Land Rover — exactly what it was built for. 2020+ D165/D180 is the family sweet spot.
Depends entirely on the journey: motorway miles suit the diesel; short urban runs are how DPFs clog and oil dilutes. Under 10 miles a day? P250 or P300e.
2,000–2,500kg capacity — fine for trailers and small caravans, but this is the point in the range where the full-size Discovery earns its money.
Cheap early diesels look tempting; budget for a chain inspection before purchase, not after. A £250 inspection against a £5,000 engine is the best odds on this page.
The gateway model — approachable costs, huge specialist coverage, and every mistake already documented. Start post-facelift and it's a genuinely easy ownership entry.
